sourceMovie.title: The Basketball Diaries
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto The Basketball Diaries (1995) as a drama theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The drama and crime blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: American Psycho
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Basketball Diaries clicked for you, American Psycho (2000) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and crime, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after The Basketball Diaries?
faq.answer: Try American Psycho, Requiem for a Dream, Trainspotting—each shares DNA with The Basketball Diaries while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is The Basketball Diaries?
faq.answer: The Basketball Diaries (1995) is best described as Drama, Crime. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is The Basketball Diaries worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.4/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this drama lane.
